items.title: Agent-oriented AxonFlow: bodies, merge import, CLI parity, search, export, validate, shell UX, docs, MCP
items.body:
**Objective:** Track implementable work so AI agents and humans can maintain rich work-item state in AxonFlow with less shell friction, fewer round-trips, and safer idempotent imports—without regressing dashboard, deps, or existing CLI JSON consumers.

**Scope in:** SQLite `Store` and `PatchItem`; `HandlersItem`, `HandlersQuery`; tests under `tests/AxonFlow.Tests`; `.cursor/skills/axonflow/SKILL.md` and optional root `AGENTS.md` pointers; optional new `src/AxonFlow.Mcp` or similar for MCP. **Scope out:** Mind-map UI (AF-85+); unrelated dashboard styling; breaking removal of `--id` on update without deprecation path.

**Implementation notes:** Prefer additive CLI options (`--ref`, `--body-file`, `--merge`) and backward-compatible defaults. `PatchItem` uses null-vs-sentinel semantics: document whether empty string clears body or is disallowed. Import merge must not create dependency cycles; reuse `HasPathSuccessorToPredecessor` checks.

**Execution:** Implement stories in parallel where deps allow; land body/store changes before import merge that patches bodies.

**Validation:** `dotnet test`; manual `axonflow validate --json`; spot-check `item next`, `dashboard emit` unchanged for snapshot schema.

**Risks:** System.CommandLine beta4 argv edge cases on Windows; duplicate clientKey semantics confusing users—document in SKILL.

**Handoff:** Read child stories in priority order; start `item next` after any predecessor story completes its blocking tasks.

items.title: Story: Work item body on add/update (Store.PatchItem + CLI)
items.body:
**Objective:** Agents can set and revise item `body` via CLI aligned with SKILL entry-quality rules, without relying solely on `item import` or notes.

**Scope:** `Store.PatchItem` extension; `item add` and `item update` options; JSON output unchanged shape aside from new fields on requests. **Out:** Rich text/HTML; collaborative editing.

**Implementation:** `Store.cs` add optional `body` patch parameter (decide: `null` = no change, empty = clear vs forbidden). `HandlersItem.cs` add `--body`, `--body-file` (UTF-8); optional `--append-body` or separate `item note`-like append—pick one and document. Ensure `ItemUpdate` passes through to `PatchItem`. `ItemAdd` insert must pass non-null body when provided.

**Steps:** (1) Design doc in task SB1. (2) Store+PatchItem. (3) Handlers. (4) Tests.

**Validation:** Tests cover add with body, update replace, dry-run; grep confirms no call sites break PatchItem signature.

**Risks:** Large bodies and command-line length—`--body-file` mitigates.

**Handoff:** Complete SB3 before import-merge story uses body patch.

items.title: Design: body patch semantics (null vs clear vs append) and CLI surface
items.body:
**Objective:** Written decision record consumable by implementers for `body` updates.

**Scope:** Semantics only; no code required in this task (planning artifact in AxonFlow body + optional follow-up note).

**Implementation:** Choose: (A) `null`/omitted = no change, empty string = clear body, (B) explicit `--clear-body` flag only. Decide append vs replace for multi-step agent updates. Align `item note add` as audit-only vs body as spec.

**Steps:** Document in this item body; list affected methods (`PatchItem`, `ItemAdd`, `ItemUpdate`).

**Validation:** Second reviewer agent can implement SB2/SB3 without reopening design.

**Risks:** Inconsistent with `assigned_to` empty-string clear—document parallel pattern.

**Handoff:** SB2 implements chosen semantics exactly.

items.title: Implement Store.PatchItem (and any SQL) for body updates
items.body:
**Objective:** Database layer can persist body changes safely with `updated_at` bump.

**Scope:** `PatchItem` signature extension; all callers updated. **Out:** Import merge (separate story).

**Implementation:** Add `string? body` parameter following chosen semantics from SB1. Update every `PatchItem` call site in repo (`HandlersItem`, `ItemStart`, defer, etc.).

**Steps:** Compile-driven fix of call sites; verify no partial updates leak.

**Validation:** `dotnet build`; small unit test if Store is testable, else via handler tests in SB4.

**Risks:** Transaction boundaries—reuse existing tx patterns.

**Handoff:** SB3 wires CLI options; run full test suite before merge.

items.title: HandlersItem: item add/update body and body-file options
items.body:
**Objective:** CLI exposes `--body`, `--body-file` (and append/clear per SB1) on `add` and `update`.

**Scope:** Options parsing, file read UTF-8, size guardrails optional (e.g. max 256KB). **Out:** Stdin `-` convention (shell story may add).

**Implementation:** Mirror patterns from `item note add --body`. `item update` should accept `--ref` when added in sibling story—coordinate merge order if same PR or sequence SR1 before mixing.

**Steps:** Options on `add`/`update`; wire to `WorkItemInsert` / `PatchItem`; JSON mode responses include updated DTO.

**Validation:** Manual CLI on Windows; `dotnet test` from SB4.

**Risks:** `--file` name clash with `import --file`—use distinct option names.

**Handoff:** SB4 adds automated coverage; SI2 may begin after this task.

items.title: Tests: body add/update, dry-run, regression on PatchItem callers
items.body:
**Objective:** Prevent regressions on body lifecycle and CLI contracts.

**Scope:** New tests invoking handlers or in-memory DB patterns used elsewhere in `AxonFlow.Tests`. **Out:** Browser/dashboard E2E.

**Implementation:** Follow `DashboardEmitTests` or item-handler test style if present; else add minimal integration tests with temp SQLite file.

**Steps:** Add cases: add with body; update body; update without body unchanged; dry-run.

**Validation:** `dotnet test` green.

**Risks:** Test flakiness from temp paths—use `Path.GetTempFileName` + dispose.

**Handoff:** Mark story ready for import-merge dependency.

items.title: Story: item import upsert/merge when clientKey exists
items.body:
**Objective:** Re-running the same import JSON with `clientKey` updates existing rows instead of silent skip, so agents can safely retry plan materialization.

**Scope:** `HandlersItem.ItemImport`; transaction behavior; JSON flag e.g. `--merge` default off for backward compatibility. **Out:** Full two-way sync of dependency graph deletion (phase 2+).

**Implementation v1:** When `--merge` and clientKey hit existing row: patch title/body/status/priority/parent via `Resolve`/IDs; skip or additive-merge deps per spec in SI1. Preserve `ref_number` stability.

**Steps:** SI1 spec; SI2 code; SI3 tests.

**Validation:** Import same file twice with changed title; DB shows one row updated; dry-run lists planned patches JSON.

**Risks:** TempId map for same import session must still work; emergent provenance rules unchanged.

**Handoff:** Requires SB3 complete for body merge path.

items.title: Spec: import --merge field matrix and dependency behavior v1
items.body:
**Objective:** Define exactly which import JSON fields update an existing `clientKey` match and how `dependencies` array behaves on merge.

**Scope:** Written spec in this body; edge cases: stream change, emergent discovered-from, parent by tempId vs id. **Out:** Implementation.

**Implementation:** Recommend v1: update title/body/status/priority/parent when provided; dependencies: only add new edges from payload, never auto-delete absent edges OR document 'payload is source of truth for deps'—pick one.

**Steps:** Enumerate matrices; align with cycle detection `HasPathSuccessorToPredecessor`.

**Validation:** Peer review via second agent.

**Risks:** Overwriting in-progress assignee—decide policy.

**Handoff:** SI2 implements spec literally; add `--dry-run` merge preview JSON.

items.title: Implement item import --merge (CLI flag + merge branch)
items.body:
**Objective:** Working `--merge` on `item import` per SI1, gated off default path.

**Scope:** CLI option registration in `CliRoot` or local to import command; merge branch uses `PatchItem` / targeted updates. **Out:** Dep deletion semantics deferred if not in SI1.

**Implementation:** Parse flag; on duplicate clientKey call merge instead of `continue`; maintain `tempMap` for tempIds pointing at existing ids.

**Steps:** Implement; log/JSON summary of merged refs for `--json`.

**Validation:** Manual two-pass import; `dotnet test`.

**Risks:** Interaction with `InsertItem` ref allocation—no duplicate inserts.

**Handoff:** SI3 adds automated tests; update SKILL import section.

items.title: Tests: import merge idempotency and dry-run preview
items.body:
**Objective:** Lock merge behavior against regressions to skip-only import.

**Scope:** Temp DB; two imports same clientKey; assert single row and updated fields. **Out:** Performance stress.

**Implementation:** Use isolated SQLite file in test output; subprocess or direct handler calls if test harness exists.

**Steps:** Happy path merge; merge off keeps old title; cycle still rejected.

**Validation:** `dotnet test`.

**Risks:** Test parallelization locking DB—unique db per test.

**Handoff:** Document example in SKILL cheat sheet.

items.title: Story: CLI identifier parity (item update --ref; item list --parent resolves AF-n)
items.body:
**Objective:** Agents using `AF-n` refs everywhere avoid extra `item show` to learn UUIDs for update/list filters.

**Scope:** `HandlersItem.ItemUpdate` option shape; `ItemList` parent resolution. **Out:** Changing global id format.

**Implementation:** `item update`: require exactly one of `--id` or `--ref` (mutually exclusive validation); resolve via existing `Resolve`. `item list --parent`: if value non-empty and not UUID-like, attempt `ResolveItem` for parent row and use its id in `ItemListQuery`.

**Steps:** SR1 update; SR2 list; SR3 tests/help text.

**Validation:** Manual `item list --parent AF-12 --json`; tests for ambiguous parent strings.

**Risks:** UUID collision with ref-like strings—unlikely; document resolution order.

**Handoff:** Can ship independently of body story.

items.title: item update: accept --ref (mutually exclusive with --id)
items.body:
**Objective:** `item update --ref AF-42` works like other commands.

**Scope:** Option `--ref` optional; validation ensures one identifier present. **Out:** Removing `--id`.

**Implementation:** Add `refOpt`; handler resolves to id before `PatchItem`; help examples.

**Steps:** Code + help; keep `--id` for scripts using UUIDs.

**Validation:** Manual + unit validation errors when both missing.

**Risks:** Breaking scripts that passed bogus id—none expected.

**Handoff:** SR2 list parent.

items.title: item list: resolve --parent AF-n via ResolveItem before query
items.body:
**Objective:** `--parent` accepts project ref prefix + number same as `item add --parent`.

**Scope:** `ItemList` only; empty `--parent` still means roots. **Out:** Other filters ref resolution.

**Implementation:** If parent string non-empty, `ResolveItem(c,pid,parent)` → use `.Id`; if null, error with clear JSON err.

**Steps:** Implement; align with Store `ItemListQuery` parent id.

**Validation:** Manual list children of a known story.

**Risks:** None significant.

**Handoff:** SR3 tests.

items.title: Tests and help text for update --ref and list --parent ref
items.body:
**Objective:** Coverage for new CLI paths and error messages (`--json`).

**Scope:** Automated tests + `--help` strings if applicable. **Out:** Localization.

**Implementation:** Assert exit codes 2 vs 3 for validation vs not found.

**Steps:** Add tests; verify JSON error shape matches `JsonOut.WriteErr` patterns.

**Validation:** `dotnet test`.

**Risks:** None.

**Handoff:** SKILL cheat sheet one-liner for `item update --ref`.

items.title: Story: item list extended filters (assignee, body contains, updated-after)
items.body:
**Objective:** Reduce `export`/`show` churn when agents search the graph.

**Scope:** `Store.ItemListQuery` + SQL; `HandlersItem` options; `--json` DTO unchanged per row. **Out:** Full-text FTS5 (optional future).

**Implementation:** Add optional filters: `--assigned-to`, `--body-contains` (LIKE), `--updated-after` ISO8601 parse to compare `updated_at`. Combine with AND semantics.

**Steps:** SL1 store+handler; SL2 tests.

**Validation:** `dotnet test`; manual query with agent assignee filter.

**Risks:** LIKE performance on large DBs—acceptable for dev tool v1.

**Handoff:** Document new flags in SKILL.

items.title: Store ListItems + HandlersItem options for new filters
items.body:
**Objective:** Filtered list queries per story scope.

**Scope:** Extend `ItemListQuery` record and SQL WHERE clauses; CLI options. **Out:** Subtree `--under-ref` unless trivial extension.

**Implementation:** Parameterized SQL; null = no filter; validate ISO parse with clear errors.

**Steps:** Implement all three filters; sort unchanged unless requested later.

**Validation:** `dotnet build`.

**Risks:** Timezone: document UTC vs local—prefer UTC Z in docs.

**Handoff:** SL2 tests.

items.title: Tests: item list new filters and SQL AND composition
items.body:
**Objective:** Prove filters narrow results and combine correctly.

**Scope:** Seed minimal rows in temp DB or use handler-level test. **Out:** E2E CLI subprocess suite.

**Implementation:** Insert items with distinct assignee/body/updated_at; assert list counts.

**Validation:** `dotnet test`.

**Risks:** Clock sensitivity—inject `Store.Now` if tests flaky (out of scope unless needed).

**Handoff:** SKILL examples.

items.title: Story: export markdown/json with bodies, notes, deps (optional subtree)
items.body:
**Objective:** Git-visible or pasteable snapshots include enough for agent handoff, not only title lines.

**Scope:** `HandlersQuery.RunExport`; optional `--root-ref` to limit subtree; notes limit flag. **Out:** PDF.

**Implementation:** Markdown: sections per item with body excerpt or full body; list deps as `AF-a -> AF-b`; last N notes per item optional `--notes-limit`. JSON path may echo `ItemJson` with embedded deps array if not already.

**Steps:** SE1 implement; SE2 tests + doc.

**Validation:** Snapshot compare or golden file in tests.

**Risks:** Export size—add `--max-body-chars` optional.

**Handoff:** SKILL mentions `export --format markdown` depth.

items.title: Implement enriched export (markdown + optional JSON parity)
items.body:
**Objective:** `export --format markdown` suitable for PR attachments.

**Scope:** Read deps via `ListDependenciesForProject`; map ids to refs; include body text; optional `--root-ref` filter using parent walk or BFS from root. **Out:** HTML export.

**Implementation:** Build StringBuilder carefully for large projects; guard memory.

**Steps:** Code flags; preserve existing json default behavior or extend with query flag.

**Validation:** Manual export on real DB sample.

**Risks:** Markdown escaping in titles/bodies—use minimal escaping for code fences risk.

**Handoff:** SE2 tests.

items.title: Tests and SKILL note for enriched export
items.body:
**Objective:** Lock markdown structure; document flags.

**Scope:** Unit test on small synthetic project in temp DB. **Out:** README full rewrite.

**Implementation:** Assert substring presence for ref, body snippet, dep line.

**Validation:** `dotnet test`.

**Risks:** None.

**Handoff:** Users can rely on export for reviews.

items.title: Story: validate exit codes, --fail-on, optional SKILL-aligned warnings
items.body:
**Objective:** Scripts and agents can gate on `validate` (CI/local) and opt into stricter policy checks.

**Scope:** `HandlersQuery.RunValidate`; exit code contract; new flags. **Out:** Auto-fix.

**Implementation:** Define: e.g. exit 0 ok; 1 warnings only if `--fail-on warnings`; 2 errors on violatedDependencies; document matrix. Optional warnings: story without child tasks, body shorter than N chars—off by default `--strict-skill`.

**Steps:** SV1 spec+impl exit codes; SV2 `--fail-on`; SV3 strict checks + tests.

**Validation:** Tests spawn validate with temp DB fixtures.

**Risks:** Breaking existing users expecting always 0—default must stay backward compatible.

**Handoff:** Document in README/SKILL.

items.title: validate: non-zero exit on selected report sections (backward compatible default)
items.body:
**Objective:** CI can fail on violated deps without parsing JSON stdout only.

**Scope:** After building report, if `violatedDependencies` non-empty set exit code !=0 when new default or flag—**must** preserve today behavior unless `--strict-exit` default change is rejected; prefer additive `--fail-on violatedDependencies` defaulting to current behavior with opt-in fail.

**Implementation:** Parse existing lists; map names to exit; document.

**Steps:** Implement flag; JSON output unchanged structure.

**Validation:** Manual `echo $?` / `%ERRORLEVEL%` on Windows cmd.

**Risks:** Shell scripts in wild—additive only.

**Handoff:** SV2 expands `--fail-on` list.

items.title: Generalize --fail-on categories (deps, stale, deep, emergent, cycles)
items.body:
**Objective:** Single flag accepting comma-separated report keys that trigger non-zero exit.

**Scope:** CLI parsing; case-insensitive keys matching JSON report properties. **Out:** YAML config file.

**Implementation:** Reuse SV1 plumbing; help text lists allowed keys.

**Steps:** Code + help; integration test.

**Validation:** `dotnet test`.

**Risks:** Typos in keys—clear error message.

**Handoff:** SV3 optional skill warnings.

items.title: Optional --strict-skill warnings (story without children, short body heuristic)
items.body:
**Objective:** Agents can surface SKILL hygiene issues without failing default validate.

**Scope:** Heuristics only; configurable thresholds via CLI ints optional. **Out:** NLP quality scoring.

**Implementation:** Query items type=story without children; body length < threshold for types in {story,task,feature}; append new report array `skillWarnings`.

**Steps:** Implement; gate non-zero only if `--fail-on skillWarnings`.

**Validation:** Tests with synthetic rows.

**Risks:** False positives on intentional minimal bodies—tune thresholds.

**Handoff:** SKILL references `--strict-skill`.

items.title: Story: stdin/body-file patterns and explicit clear-assignee
items.body:
**Objective:** Windows/PowerShell agents avoid fragile `""` (empty-string argv) and huge `-body` argv.

**Scope:** `item note add`, `item add`/`update` once body story lands: support reading body from stdin when `--body -` or dedicated `--stdin-body`; `--clear-assignee` boolean on update. **Out:** POSIX-only tools.

**Implementation:** Shared helper `ReadBodyFromFileOrStdin`; document UTF-8 BOM handling; `PatchItem` already clears assignee with empty string—expose CLI.

**Steps:** SH1 helper + note add; SH2 update/add integration; SH3 tests.

**Validation:** PowerShell sample in SKILL; `dotnet test`.

**Risks:** Console encoding—document `chcp 65001` if needed.

**Handoff:** Coordinate release with body story.

items.title: Shared helper: read text from --body-file or stdin (UTF-8)
items.body:
**Objective:** One code path for file/stdin body ingestion with size limits.

**Scope:** New small static class or method in existing handler file; max bytes constant. **Out:** Streaming parser.

**Implementation:** `File.ReadAllText` async optional; stdin `Console.In.ReadToEndAsync` with caution for interactive—document non-interactive use.

**Steps:** Implement; unit test stdin tricky—prefer file-based test + mock stream if available.

**Validation:** Unit tests where feasible.

**Risks:** Hanging on stdin—document agent usage 'pipe file content'.

**Handoff:** SH2 wires to commands.

items.title: item update: --clear-assignee flag and note add body-file
items.body:
**Objective:** No empty-string argv needed to clear assignee; notes can append from file.

**Scope:** `ItemUpdate` passes assignee clear when flag true; `item note add` gains `--body-file`. **Out:** Other commands.

**Implementation:** `--clear-assignee` calls `PatchItem` with assignedTo empty or dedicated store method—match existing empty semantics.

**Steps:** Options + handler; help text.

**Validation:** Manual + tests.

**Risks:** Conflicts if both `--assigned-to` and clear—validate mutual exclusion.

**Handoff:** SH3 tests.

items.title: Tests: clear-assignee, body-file on note add, error cases
items.body:
**Objective:** Coverage for shell-oriented flags.

**Scope:** Automated tests only. **Out:** Manual PS1 script in repo.

**Implementation:** Temp files with unicode content; assert note stored.

**Validation:** `dotnet test`.

**Risks:** None.

**Handoff:** SKILL Windows examples updated.

items.title: Story: SKILL and repo guidance for agents (tree --json, db path, AGENTS.md)
items.body:
**Objective:** Agents discover `tree --json`, explicit `--db`, and optional repo `AGENTS.md` without reading source.

**Scope:** `.cursor/skills/axonflow/SKILL.md`; optional new `AGENTS.md` at repo root with short AxonFlow subsection. **Out:** Product code.

**Implementation:** Add cheat-sheet lines for `tree --json`, `export` after SE*; document `disable-model-invocation` implication and recommend user rule or AGENTS pointer.

**Steps:** SD1 SKILL edit; SD2 AGENTS optional + cross-link.

**Validation:** Human read-through; no `dotnet test` required.

**Risks:** SKILL length—keep concise.

**Handoff:** User enables skill or rule in Cursor.

items.title: Update SKILL.md: tree --json, list filters (after SL*), export, validate flags
items.body:
**Objective:** SKILL reflects shipped CLI from sibling stories.

**Scope:** Documentation only; time after SL/SE/SV land or mark sections 'upcoming'—prefer landing doc PR after features.

**Implementation:** Add subsection Hierarchy inspection; link to `validate` strict modes.

**Steps:** Edit markdown; review for Windows paths.

**Validation:** N/A CI.

**Risks:** Doc drift if merged before code—sequence PRs or single doc PR at end.

**Handoff:** SD2 AGENTS.

items.title: Optional AGENTS.md (repo root) AxonFlow quickstart for Cursor agents
items.body:
**Objective:** Repo-local instruction file points agents to `--db .axonflow/axonflow.db` or user profile path pattern.

**Scope:** Short file if team wants it; Cursor picks up AGENTS.md automatically in many setups. **Out:** Enterprise policy docs.

**Implementation:** 15–30 lines: when to use AxonFlow; link to skill; three canonical commands.

**Steps:** Add file if product owner agrees; else cancel task with note.

**Validation:** Open in Cursor and confirm agent sees it.

**Risks:** Duplicate guidance with SKILL—keep DRY.

**Handoff:** Close story.

items.title: Story: AxonFlow MCP server for Cursor (tools wrapping core workflows)
items.body:
**Objective:** Cursor agents invoke AxonFlow without shell quoting via MCP tools (`item_next`, `item_show`, `item_start`, `validate`, `import_dry_run`, etc.).

**Scope:** Separate package or `src/AxonFlow.Mcp` host using official MCP C# SDK or stdio bridge to existing CLI—decide in SM1. **Out:** Hosted SaaS.

**Implementation:** Thin tools delegating to `Store`/`Handlers*` static methods if refactored for reuse, or process spawn `axonflow` with `--json` (simpler v1, heavier).

**Steps:** SM1 spike; SM2 project layout + DI; SM3 tool map; SM4 docs install cursor mcp.json.

**Validation:** Manual Cursor tool call; minimal automated test for stdio protocol if feasible.

**Risks:** MCP API churn; security of DB path exposure—document local-only.

**Handoff:** Users add MCP server config once.

items.title: Spike: MCP hosting approach (in-process Store vs CLI subprocess)
items.body:
**Objective:** Decision record with recommendation and prototype snippet.

**Scope:** Read Cursor MCP docs; evaluate maintenance. **Out:** Full server.

**Implementation:** Prototype one tool end-to-end; measure cold start.

**Steps:** Document pros/cons in this item body.

**Validation:** Peer sign-off.

**Risks:** In-process duplicates Store logic vs single library—consider extracting `AxonFlow.Core` library later (out of scope unless spike proves need).

**Handoff:** SM2 implements chosen path.

items.title: Scaffold MCP host project and solution wiring
items.body:
**Objective:** Buildable MCP server project in solution.

**Scope:** csproj, Program.cs stdio entry, package references per SM1. **Out:** Published NuGet.

**Implementation:** Follow MCP SDK template; `--db` from tool args or env `AXONFLOW_DB`.

**Steps:** Add project; `dotnet sln add`; README section stub.

**Validation:** `dotnet build` solution.

**Risks:** TFMs must align with SDK.

**Handoff:** SM3 tools.

items.title: Implement initial MCP tools (next, show, start, note add, validate, import dry-run)
items.body:
**Objective:** Cover the SKILL loop without shell.

**Scope:** Six tools minimum; JSON schemas for inputs/outputs mirroring CLI `--json`. **Out:** dashboard emit from MCP.

**Implementation:** Map to handler methods or CLI; consistent error objects.

**Steps:** Implement tools; register; manual Cursor test each.

**Validation:** Checklist in task notes.

**Risks:** Timeout on long imports—stream or limit.

**Handoff:** SM4 user docs.

items.title: Document Cursor MCP config snippet and security guidance (local DB only)
items.body:
**Objective:** Users can enable server in one copy-paste.

**Scope:** README or `docs/mcp.md`; example `mcpServers` JSON. **Out:** Marketplace distribution.

**Implementation:** Windows/macOS paths; env vars; never commit secrets.

**Steps:** Write doc; link from SKILL Remote section optional.

**Validation:** Fresh machine walkthrough.

**Risks:** Cursor UI changes—screenshot optional.

**Handoff:** Close MCP story.
